items.title: Class action claims women were raped and stalked at remote Fortescue mines

children.content: Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest's mining giant Fortescue has been slapped with a landmark class action alleging systemic sexual harassment, discrimination and toxic work sites.

items.title: 'Respected' 76yo pilot killed in plane crash built replica Spitfire himself

image.altText: Two men piloting a light aircraft wearing headsets

children.content: The friend of 76-year-old Malcolm Vivian, who died flying a light plane he built himself near Northam, describes his mate as a "very well-liked pilot" and "respected aircraft builder".

items.title: Coastal town braces for economic pain as mine closure to cost 100 jobs

children.content: Mineral Resources's decision to close the Lucky Bay Garnet Mine will see 110 jobs lost in and around Kalbarri.

items.title: Perth's house price surge to continue as Sydney faces $100,000-plus drop

children.content: House price rises of up to 9 per cent in Perth have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain, but it's a far different story in Sydney and Melbourne.

items.title: Remote community's decade-long wait for clean drinking water

children.content: Residents of Pandanus Park continue their near-daily 600-metre walk to access safe drinking water in a decade-long wait for a solution to dangerous nitrate in their tap water in Western Australia's north.

items.title: Tech billionaire accused of assaulting partner, cutting $66k Birkin bag with scissors

items.title: Deadly bird flu discovered in second state, suspected case in Dunsborough

children.content: A third confirmed case of the deadly H5N1 bird flu has been found in a migratory bird on Australia's southern coast, as another suspected case emerges in WA.

articles.title: Wildlife carers 'ground zero' for third H5 bird flu case

children.content: A South Australian wildlife rescue group is working with local authorities after Australia's third confirmed case of H5 bird flu was detected in a migratory bird.

image.altText: A giant petrel bird hides in green seaside foliage at Knights Beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula.

articles.title: Australia's mammals are at risk of bird flu. This is what we know

children.content: While birds are the most likely victims of the deadly strain of bird flu, researchers are also sounding the alarm for native mammals and marsupial species. These are some of the steps being taken to protect them.

image.altText: small black animal with white stripe across chest looking at straight at the viewer, standing over dead animal

articles.title: PNG reverses ban on Australian chicken and egg imports, but keeps WA restrictions

children.content: Australia's largest chicken meat buyer, Papua New Guinea, has reversed its decision to suspend
Australian poultry and egg imports due to the H5N1 bird flu outbreak.

articles.title: ASIO reveals new details on AUKUS espionage, warns of 'unprecedented threats'

items.title: Who is going to qualify for the World Cup knockout rounds?

children.content: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

items.title: Bird flu has arrived in Australia

children.content: After years of waiting, the deadly H5N1 strain of bird flu has finally arrived in Australia.
Two birds found on the southern coast of Western Australia near Esperance have been confirmed as infected, but there is no evidence so far of a mass-mortality event.
So, what actually is H5N1, and why does it matter?
